text: This video will demonstrate the anatomy of the left chest.
Here with the lung reflected inferiorly, you see the arch of the aorta and the left main pulmonary artery.
Posteriorly, you can also see that pulmonary artery towards the apex.
Or inferiorly, you'll see the inferior ligament with the reflection of the lymph nodes through there.
And here we're going to point out the inferior pulmonary vein.
And just to add to that is the left main bronchus.
Here in a separate case, when you divide the inferior pulmonary ligament, this will take you up to the inferior pulmonary vein, which is that vascular structure there.
Posteriorly, we're looking at the dissected posterior hilum.
And here you see, again, the left main stem bronchus.
and the pulmonary artery posteriorly.
Here in the fissure, we see a nice demonstration of the branching pulmonary artery.
In the foreground, you see the lingular artery headed north.
In the background, you see a branch to the posterior upper lobe, as well as the superior segment pulmonary artery.
Next, we're going to reflect the lung posteriorly.

Towards the apex of the chest, we can examine the thoracic inlet.

The first intercostal muscle between the first and second ribs.
